package.title: YKA ..-30, YDA ..-30 Star-Delta Starters — Open Type Version — Protection by Thermal O/L Relay
package.subtitle: Wiring Diagrams
package.evidence: Title block: 'YKA ..-30, YDA ..-30 Star-Delta Starters / Open Type Version / Protection by Thermal O/L Relay'; footer '2/4', '1SBC 0095 00 R1001', 'ABB Control'; section labels 'Power Circuit', 'Local Control', 'Remote Control'.

devices.detail: Star (Y) contactor — coil terminals A1/A2, three main poles (1/L1-2/T1, 3/L2-4/T2, 5/L3-6/T3), aux contacts 13-14 (NO) and 21-22 (NC). Placed under 'Y' in control diagrams.
devices.evidence: KM2 with A1/A2, poles 1/L1..6/T3, aux 13,14,21,22; coil KM2 under 'Y' at bottom of control circuits.

devices.detail: Delta (△) contactor — coil terminals A1/A2, three main poles (1/L1-2/T1, 3/L2-4/T2, 5/L3-6/T3), aux contact 21-22 (NC). Placed under '△' in control diagrams; feeds control supply reference points KM3:3/L2 and KM3:5/L3.
devices.evidence: KM3 with A1/A2, poles 1/L1..6/T3, aux 21,22; coil KM3 under '△'; 'KM3 : 5/L3' and 'KM3 : 3/L2' labels in control circuits.

devices.detail: Line/main (L) contactor — coil terminals A1/A2, three main poles (1/L1-2/T1, 3/L2-4/T2, 5/L3-6/T3) feeding overload FR1, aux contacts 13-14 (NO) and 23-24 (NO). Placed under 'L' in control diagrams.
devices.evidence: KM1 with A1/A2, poles 1/L1..6/T3, aux 13,14,23,24; coil KM1 under 'L'.

devices.detail: Thermal O/L relay in the motor (delta) current path — heater elements on 2/T1, 4/T2, 6/T3; signalling contacts 95-96 (NC) and 97-98 (NO), with Reset (R) and Auto/Manual (O/R) selection shown.
devices.evidence: FR1 with 95,96,97,98, terminals 2/T1,4/T2,6/T3, and 'R'/'O/R' reset symbols.

devices.detail: Star-delta timing relay — coil A1/A2, timed changeover contacts marked 15/16/18 with delays t1 and t1+t2 (t1 = star time, t2 = transition). Controls the Y→△ transition.
Three-phase induction motor, winding terminals U1/V1/W1 and U2/V2/W2 for star-delta connection. Two motor symbols drawn to show the terminal-box connection arrangement.

conductors.detail: Three-phase incoming supply bussed to line side (1/L1, 3/L2, 5/L3) of the contactors; no printed wire numbers on this diagram.
conductors.evidence: Three horizontal bus lines feeding contactor line terminals in power circuit.

conductors.detail: Contactor/overload load terminals (2/T1,4/T2,6/T3) route to motor winding terminals U1/V1/W1 and U2/V2/W2 for star/delta switching; no printed wire numbers.
conductors.evidence: Dashed connection lines between power terminals and motor terminals.

contacts.detail: Line/main contactor main contacts feeding FR1
Overload trip NC contact used in control circuit
contacts.evidence: 95/96 at FR1 in power and control circuits

contacts.detail: Timer changeover: after t1 star drops, after t1+t2 delta picks up (Y→△ transition)

power_domains.detail: Motor power domain — no voltage value printed on this sheet.
Control circuit fed from Us and N; Us reference derived from KM3:5/L3 in both control diagrams. No voltage value printed.

off_page_references.detail: Control-supply feed point referenced to KM3 power terminal 5/L3 in Power Circuit; appears at top of both Local and Remote control diagrams beside 'Us'.
Control-return point referenced to KM3 power terminal 3/L2; appears at bottom of both control diagrams beside 'N'.

functional_paths.name: Delta (△) run
sequence: KM3 main (connects U2/V2/W2 to phases for delta)

unresolved.resolution: Confirm Us and motor voltage from the sheet 1/4 or 4/4 rating table / order data before assuming a level.

cross_sheet_notes: This is sheet 2/4 of ABB doc 1SBC 0095 00 R1001. Diagram IDs E1127D (power), E1612D (local control) and E1128D (remote control) identify the three sub-drawings. Control supply reference points KM3:5/L3 (Us) and KM3:3/L2 (N) tie the control circuits back to the power circuit contactor terminals.

brief.sheet_title: Sheet 2/4 of an ABB open-type star-delta motor starter (YKA/YDA ..-30) — the wiring diagrams for the power circuit plus two control-circuit options (local and remote), protected by a thermal overload relay.
brief.purpose: This sheet shows how to wire a star-delta (wye-delta) motor starter. Three contactors switch the motor: KM1 is the line/main contactor (marked 'L'), KM2 is the star contactor (marked 'Y'), and KM3 is the delta contactor (marked '△'). To limit inrush at start, the motor windings are first connected in star (KM1 + KM2). A timing relay KT then times out and hands over to delta (KM1 + KM3) for normal running. The thermal overload relay FR1 sits in the motor current path and its 95-96 trip contact opens the control circuit on overload. The bottom two diagrams give two ways to build the control side: 'Local Control' (start/reset pushbuttons on the panel) and 'Remote Control' (start/stop/reset from a remote station). The control supply is taken as Us/N, with reference points tied to KM3 terminals 5/L3 and 3/L2.
brief.troubleshooting_example: Symptom: motor will not start from the Local Control station. With the circuit safely de-energized and locked out, check the overload path first: put a meter (continuity) across FR1 95-96 — it should read closed when the relay is not tripped. If 95-96 is open, the overload has tripped (reset it / investigate the motor load) — the fault is at the device. If 95-96 reads closed but the KM1 coil (A1-A2) never energizes when the start button is pressed, work along the control string toward the coil: verify the start pushbutton and the KM1 23-24 seal-in contact, and confirm control supply is present at the Us reference (KM3:5/L3) versus N (KM3:3/L2). If good voltage/continuity reaches the start-button side but nothing appears at the KM1 coil, the break is in the wiring/contacts between them, not in the overload relay. Note: this sheet shows only the starter's own control logic — there is no PLC/controller logic on this drawing, so no external control response can be inferred here.
brief.safety_context: No voltage value is printed anywhere on this sheet — the drawing does NOT establish the control-circuit or motor voltage, so do not assume a level. For continuity/resistance checks on FR1 contacts, contactor coils or the timer, de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first. If live verification of the control supply (Us/N) or the three-phase power terminals is required, treat it as energized work and use appropriately rated PPE and instruments; the drawing does not prove the present field state of the panel.
unresolved_items: Supply/control voltage not printed on this sheet (check the rating/order-data sheet 1/4 or 4/4).
FR1 97-98 contact and its destination not clearly legible.
KT timer contact terminal routing (15/16/18, t1 vs t1+t2) is small and needs a zoom to confirm.
No explicit -W#### wire numbers appear on this wiring diagram; connections are shown graphically only.
